content.problemStatement:
Alice and Bob are playing a game.

First, Alice throws NNN darts at a board with dimensions 109×10910^9 \times 10^9109×109 at random. Each dart hits the board at a point (some of these points may coincide). Then, Bob should answer Alice's QQQ questions. In each question, Alice chooses two integers lll and rrr; Bob should only consider all points hit in throws lll through rrr (inclusive) and compute the distance between the farthest pair of these points.

Help Bob find the answers to all queries.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains a single integer N denoting the number of points. N lines follow with two space-separated integers xi and yi denoting the coordinates of the point hit with the i-th dart. The next line contains a single integer Q denoting the number of queries. Each of the following Q lines contains two space-separated integers l and r describing one query.
content.outputFormat: For each query, print a single line containing one integer — the square of the maximum distance.

sampleTestCases.explanation: The two points are (1,1) and (2,2). The distance between them is sqrt((2-1)^2 + (2-1)^2) = sqrt(2). The square of this distance is 2.
The three points are (1,1), (2,2), and (3,3). The maximum distance is between (1,1) and (3,3), which is sqrt((3-1)^2 + (3-1)^2) = sqrt(8). The square of this distance is 8.

solution.approach: To solve this problem, we first read the points and store them in an array. Then, for each query, we calculate the maximum distance between any two points in the given range. We use the distance formula: sqrt((x2-x1)^2 + (y2-y1)^2) to calculate the distance between two points. We keep track of the maximum distance found so far and update it if a larger distance is found. Finally, we print the square of the maximum distance for each query.
solution.timeComplexity: O(Q*N^2) — for each query, we potentially check all pairs of points in the given range
solution.spaceComplexity: O(N) — we store all points in an array
